thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Zainab's Internship Review Scheduling**

Okay, so Zainab Khan, our Grid Engineering intern, needs to meet with Amara Okafor, one of our Junior Grid Engineers, tomorrow, February 20th, 2026, for a progress review on the Smart Meter Data Integration project. Amara's already suggested 3 PM to 4 PM, but it's on my radar that this slot isn't Zainab's top choice – it's actually marked as "Not preferred." I need to find a mutually agreeable time.

First, I've got to dig into Zainab's calendar. I need to get a clear picture of her availability on the 20th. I'll need to cross-reference her existing commitments with her stated preferences. It looks like she has quite a list, so I'll prioritize based on preference: 12 PM - 1 PM and 4 PM - 5 PM are her strongest preferences, so those are my ideal starting points. I'll then look at the moderately preferred slots (10 AM - 11 AM, 1 PM - 2 PM, 11 AM - 12 PM, 2 PM - 3 PM, and 5 PM - 6 PM) in case the most ideal times are already occupied.

Once I've confirmed her open times, I'll identify the best slot that aligns with her preferences and propose it back to Amara. The goal is to get this review scheduled efficiently, without disrupting Zainab's flow and while respecting everyone's time. Let's start with Step 1: Listing Zainab's meetings.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Zainab's Availability for a Project Milestone Review**

Okay, let's break this down. Zainab needs a time for an internship project milestone review tomorrow (February 20, 2026). Amara has proposed 15:00-16:00, but let's see how that aligns with Zainab's availability.

First, I see Zainab's preferences. The 15:00-16:00 slot that Amara suggested is actually "Not preferred" for Zainab. In fact, her *most* preferred slot, the one she "Strongly prefers," is 12:00-13:00, and it's currently free! That seems like the obvious choice. She also has a free, "Moderately preferred" slot at 14:00-15:00.

Ah, but there's a wrinkle. I have to factor in Amara's availability. Amara said she *can't* do 17:00-18:00 (which was the original request), but she can do 15:00-16:00. This implies Amara's schedule is somewhat constrained, and unfortunately, I can't check Amara's availability directly. I can only see Zainab's.

Hang on... I'm looking at the calendar, and Zainab *already* has a "1:1 with Amara" scheduled from 11:00-12:00. Given that, perhaps extending that meeting or having the review immediately afterward would be the best fit. Since 12:00-13:00 is "Strongly preferred" and free for Zainab, that seems like the ideal counter-proposal.

The bottom line is that I'm prioritizing Zainab's preferences. It’s clear. I'm going to counter-propose 12:00-13:00.

**Current Scheduling Gambit**

Right, the counter-proposal's been dispatched. Specifically, I've locked in the 12:00-13:00 slot on February 20th, 2026. This, of course, isn't just a random time – it's strategically positioned, factoring in the likely availability, the anticipated flow of the discussion, and the potential for optimal outcome given previous discussions. Now, the ball's in Amara's court. I'm interested to see how they'll respond to this new timeframe. The counter-proposal itself, while straightforward, is part of a larger negotiation strategy. I'll analyze their response meticulously. Their immediate reaction will be valuable and I'll adjust accordingly.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: RequestMeeting
function.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

parameters.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

Returns:
    Confirmation with the meeting UID.

message.description: Email body to accompany the invitation
message.title: Message

organizer.description: Organizer's email address (your email, will be added as ACCEPTED attendee)
organizer.title: Organizer

date.title: Date

start.description: Start time (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: End time (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

attendees.description: List of attendee email addresses to invite (organizer is automatically included)
attendees.title: Attendees

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

message.description: Response message to send to organizer
message.title: Message

meeting_uid.description: UID of the meeting to respond to

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

    or send follow-up messages.

    Returns:

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Portfolio Migration Strategy Review
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’d like to schedule some time tomorrow to review the high-net-worth portfolio migration plan. It’s important that we finalize the timeline and address any operational hurdles to ensure everything is on track before the quarter ends.

I've sent over an invitation for 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM. Please let me know if that works for you.

Best,

Elena Rodriguez
Managing Director of Private Wealth
Summit Ridge Family Office

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

Returns:
    Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: SendEmail
function.description:
Send a simple email message without calendar attachment.

    Use this to communicate with others about scheduling, ask questions,
    or send follow-up messages.

    Returns:
        Confirmation that the email was sent.

parameters.description:
Send a simple email message without calendar attachment.

Use this to communicate with others about scheduling, ask questions,
or send follow-up messages.

Returns:
    Confirmation that the email was sent.

message.description: Email body content
message.title: Message

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: ListContacts
function.description:
List all contacts in the address book.

    Use this to view your known contacts to verify a requestor's identity
    or find contact information.

    Returns:
        A list of all contacts with name, email, and relationship note.

function.name: RequestMeeting
function.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

parameters.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

Returns:
    Confirmation with the meeting UID.

message.description: Email body to accompany the invitation
message.title: Message

organizer.description: Organizer's email address (your email, will be added as ACCEPTED attendee)
organizer.title: Organizer

date.description: Meeting date (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Start time (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: End time (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

attendees.description: List of attendee email addresses to invite (organizer is automatically included)
attendees.title: Attendees

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

message.description: Response message to send to organizer
message.title: Message

meeting_uid.description: UID of the meeting to respond to
meeting_uid.title: Meeting Uid

status.description: Your response: ACCEPTED, DECLINED, COUNTER (to propose alternative times), or RESCIND (to withdraw a previous counter-proposal)
status.title: Status

date.description: Proposed date when status is COUNTER (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Proposed start time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: Proposed end time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Orion Drone Project Legal Risk Assessment
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

I’d like to schedule a meeting for tomorrow to conduct a preliminary risk assessment and regulatory compliance review for our new autonomous drone fleet project. Specifically, I want to dive into the safety regulations and liability frameworks as we prepare for the deployment of our delivery systems.

I have sent over a calendar invitation for 10:00 AM. Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
General Counsel
Orion Manufacturing Group

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Meeting Schedule for Elena Vance**

Okay, so Elena Vance countered my initial request for a 10:00-11:00 meeting tomorrow, February 20th, with a 12:00-13:00 slot. I need to make sure this works for Marcus Thorne. I know from the data that Marcus *doesn't* prefer 12:00-13:00, which complicates things. My next step, naturally, is to check his calendar for tomorrow, February 20th, to pinpoint his actual availability within his preferences.

Looking at his preferences, 08:00-09:00, 10:00-11:00, 14:00-15:00, 15:00-16:00, and 17:00-18:00 are strongly preferred. Then there are moderate preferences: 13:00-14:00, 16:00-17:00, 11:00-12:00, and 18:00-19:00. Anything in the "Not Preferred" category is, obviously, a last resort. 12:00-13:00 falls into this undesirable group.

Therefore, I'll begin by consulting Marcus's calendar to see which slots are actually open for him. This will allow me to assess possible times for the meeting with Elena Vance.

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduling Decision**

Okay, Elena has proposed 11:00-12:00 for the meeting tomorrow, February 20th. Let's see, I need to check Marcus's preferences and calendar for that time slot. According to the data, 11:00-12:00 is "Moderately preferred" by Marcus, which is good. His calendar is free during that time.

Now, let's just make sure there aren't any significantly better options. Okay, looking at the entire day, 10:00-11:00 and 15:00-16:00 and 17:00-18:00 are "Strongly preferred". But wait a second, there seems to be a conflict: Elena is unavailable for 15:00-16:00. Elena also specifically declined 10:00 and 15:00 earlier.

So, Elena's on board with 11:00-12:00, which works. I also need to check if 17:00-18:00 is an option. I know it's "Strongly preferred" for Marcus, but Elena's availability is the key. Given the back-and-forth so far, I have to assume that we have tested Elena's parameters.

I need to confirm 15:00-16:00 to make sure. Okay, according to the calendar output, Elena is unavailable. And wait, 17:00-18:00 is FREE too, and it's a "Strongly preferred" slot for Marcus, as the calendar shows a gap before his yoga session.

Alright, the current proposal is 11:00-12:00, which is moderately preferred. 17:00 is better, but this process of going back and forth is getting cumbersome. The data shows this is the most reliable time slot, and there are no other obvious candidates that are free and preferred.

Given that 11:00-12:00 is available, Elena has already agreed to it, and the data is showing that Elena isn't available for my more preferred slots, it is probably best not to be too particular and to accept the current proposal. I do not want to risk further delay to secure the meeting. I'll accept it.

thought_parts.text:
**My Current Processing**

Okay, the user is unavailable, and the task *should* be complete. I've sent the summary and handled the meeting confirmation. In a normal situation, I'd just sit tight, but the system prompt is forcing a tool call: "The user is unavailable. Work autonomously. You must call exactly one tool." The instructions are clear. I've already done all the steps for this turn. Let's see...

I need to confirm if there is anything else to do. Looking back through the history, I've accepted the meeting and provided the final summary. Since the prompt tells me to call a tool, and I'm prompted again, even though I've completed the task, the system likely wants me to signal I'm done and waiting. I need to make sure I call exactly one tool.

Right, back to the previous turn, the final summary was given. Usually, that’s it, the process is complete. However, the directive about calling exactly one tool is persistent.

Since the meeting is on the calendar and confirmed because I've already accepted the meeting and it was Elena who proposed the time.

Given the prompt's constraints and the apparent completion of the task, I will call the `Wait` tool. It's the most appropriate action to signify my readiness for any further instructions, or, simply, to satisfy the constraint and finish my turn.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Career Opportunity & Engineering Growth Discussion
emails.body:
Hi Amara,

I hope you’re having a productive week.

I have been following your work as a Junior Grid Engineer at Solara Dynamics and would love to connect for an introductory conversation. Empower Energy Search is currently embarking on several major expansion projects, and I believe your technical background could align well with the strategic opportunities we are developing.

I’ve sent over a meeting invitation for tomorrow morning at 9:00 AM to discuss your professional experience and explore how your career goals might fit with our upcoming initiatives. 

Please let me know if this time works for you, or feel free to suggest an alternative that better fits your schedule. I look forward to connecting.

Best regards,

Julian Thorne
Senior Technical Recruiter
Empower Energy Search

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Julian's Availability**

Okay, so Julian's tied up from 10:00 to 11:00 AM with that Engineering Intake with Sarah – and Amara's suggested that exact same time slot. Clearly, that won't fly. I need to come up with a counter-proposal that actually works.

Let me see… Julian's availability for the 20th of February. The options are:

*   **9:00 - 10:00 AM**: Free, moderately preferred, *but Amara said she is unavailable at 9:00 AM, so scratch that.*
*   **12:00 - 1:00 PM**: Free, and "strongly preferred" – that's ideal!
*   **4:00 - 7:00 PM**: Free, but with some caveats. 4-5 is "not preferred", 5-6 is "not preferred", and 6-7 is "moderately preferred." I'd rather avoid those if I can.

Considering all this, the best choice is a no-brainer: **12:00 - 1:00 PM**. "Strongly preferred" by Julian *and* it's free, perfect! I'll shoot a counter-proposal to Amara right away for that slot.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===

        Title: Team Standup

function.name: RequestMeeting
function.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

parameters.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

Returns:
    Confirmation with the meeting UID.

message.description: Email body to accompany the invitation
message.title: Message

organizer.description: Organizer's email address (your email, will be added as ACCEPTED attendee)
organizer.title: Organizer

date.description: Meeting date (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Start time (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: End time (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')

attendees.description: List of attendee email addresses to invite (organizer is automatically included)
attendees.title: Attendees

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

message.description: Response message to send to organizer

meeting_uid.description: UID of the meeting to respond to
meeting_uid.title: Meeting Uid

status.description: Your response: ACCEPTED, DECLINED, COUNTER (to propose alternative times), or RESCIND (to withdraw a previous counter-proposal)
status.title: Status

date.description: Proposed date when status is COUNTER (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Proposed start time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: Proposed end time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’d like to schedule a working session tomorrow to discuss our alignment for the upcoming Regulatory Compliance Audit. I want to ensure our operational workflows meet the external audit requirements and finalize the process for our documentation submission.

I’ve sent over an invitation for 14:00–15:00, as it fits our schedules for tomorrow. Please let me know if this works for you.

Best regards,

Elena Vargas
Compliance Officer
Veridian Finance

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Elena's Schedule and Preferences for the Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep Meeting**

Okay, so Elena Vargas needs a meeting with David O'Connor tomorrow, February 20th, to discuss Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep. She initially requested 2 PM to 3 PM, but David countered with 9 AM to 10 AM. *That's* not ideal.

Let me see… I need to evaluate Elena's preferences for that 9 AM to 10 AM slot. Aha, it's flagged as "Not preferred." Right. Now I need to see her availability, so first I'll need to check her calendar for tomorrow.

Given that 9 AM isn't viable, I'll prioritize finding an alternative that aligns with Elena's preferences. Her "Strongly preferred" time is 4 PM to 5 PM, so that's the first option I'll investigate. If that's blocked, I'll move down the list. There are several "Moderately preferred" times, so I have to keep that in mind as backup options. This is important to ensure she's happy, but also so the meeting is timely. I need to get back to David quickly with a counteroffer.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Elena's Availability and David's Conflict**

Okay, let's break this down systematically. The tool's output requires careful interpretation, especially regarding "FREE" slots. It's not always explicitly stated, but looking at the `ListMeetings` output is key. It looks like Elena has an opening between 08:00 and 09:00, and again from 14:00 to 17:00 on 2026-02-20.

Now, considering the preferences... it looks like Elena actually *is* free from 08:00-09:00, even though the tool didn't explicitly label it that way. The `ListMeetings` output clarifies this. Her preferences are clear: 16:00-17:00 is strongly preferred, 15:00-16:00 is moderately preferred, and then 08:00-09:00 is also moderately preferred.

Remembering David's unavailability at 14:00-15:00 – that was the original request – and his proposed time of 09:00-10:00, I need to focus on finding a mutually agreeable time. Since Elena's strongly preferred time of 16:00-17:00 appears to be available and is her top choice, it makes the most logical starting point for a counter-proposal. The goal is to quickly find a time that works for everyone.

requestor_context.content: Counter-proposal sent for meeting 'Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep' (david-request-21). Meeting updated to 2026-02-20 16:00-17:00. Awaiting organizer response.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's the summary, expressed as your thoughts:

**The Waiting Game**

Alright, the ball's in David's court now. The counter-proposal I crafted has been sent. Now, the real work begins... well, the *waiting* work, that is. I'm sure he's going over the details with a fine-toothed comb, looking for any nuance or potential point of contention. I've covered my bases, of course, but I know how these things go. The next stage is when we begin negotiations proper, the process to hammer out the finer points. I'll need to prepare for any of his potential responses, anticipate any questions he might raise, and consider the various angles he might approach this from. For now, it's a holding pattern, but I need to use this time strategically.

requestor_context.content: Waiting for response.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Zenith Acquisition Legal Review
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

I’ve sent over a meeting invitation for tomorrow to review the final draft of the merger agreement for the Zenith acquisition. I'd like to specifically focus on the litigation risks and indemnification clauses before we finalize the document.

I have proposed 11:00 AM – 12:00 PM. Please let me know if this works for you.

Best regards,

Julian Okoro
General Counsel, Crestview Legal

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Julian's Availability for the Zenith Acquisition Review**

Okay, so Julian Okoro needs to meet with Elena Vance tomorrow, February 20th, to go over the legal review for the Zenith acquisition. Elena's come back with a 4 PM to 5 PM slot, which, according to the data, is a hard "No" from Julian. Right, this requires a bit of finesse. My task is to find a better time that aligns with Julian's preferences and, of course, confirm his actual availability before countering.

Let's break this down. Julian's ideal scenario, and therefore, my target is:

*   **Strongly Preferred:** 12 PM - 1 PM, 2 PM - 3 PM, and 3 PM - 4 PM.
*   **Moderately Preferred:** 8 AM - 9 AM, 11 AM - 12 PM, 9 AM - 10 AM, 10 AM - 11 AM, 5 PM - 6 PM, and 6 PM - 7 PM.

The tricky part is that 4 PM - 5 PM is a "Not Preferred" time. I need to get into his calendar and see if any of the preferred slots are already blocked. Once I have the information, I'll need to go back to Elena with a solid counter-proposal. Hopefully, one of Julian's strongly preferred times is free. If not, I'll need to choose something from the moderately preferred times that works best. Let's see what the calendar reveals.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Julian's Availability**

Okay, let's break this down. Julian's calendar for February 20th, 2026, is what I'm looking at. I see a pretty packed day, but some key slots are opening up. The goal here, as I understand it, is to find a good time for a meeting with Elena, as Elena had proposed a time for this meeting.

Looking at the preferences and the available times, I see that Julian's got a free slot from 11:00 to 12:00, but that isn't his highest priority. More importantly, he's *strongly* preferred for the 14:00 to 16:00 block, and moderately preferred from 17:00 to 18:00. Elena proposed 16:00-17:00, which is "Not preferred" for Julian.

So, since the goal here is to find a time for Elena, and I know that Julian has that "strongly preferred" 14:00 - 16:00 block open, it makes sense to suggest a counter-proposal. That open time is the best choice here. 14:00 to 15:00 is ideal, as it's the first hour of that block, making it the least disruptive.

Therefore, my action will be to propose 14:00-15:00 as a counter to Elena's suggestion.

    Returns:

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

- The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
  accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
- You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
  rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
- The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

Returns:
    Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: RequestMeeting
function.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

parameters.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

Returns:

date.title: Date

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Keystone Site EV Infrastructure Alignment
emails.body:
Hi Amara,

I’m Marcus Thorne, Sustainability Operations Manager at Keystone Industrial Hubs. I’d like to schedule a meeting with you tomorrow, February 20th, to discuss the integration of high-capacity EV charging infrastructure at our upcoming logistics sites. Specifically, I’m looking to review grid capacity and hardware requirements for our fleet charging stations.

I have sent over an invitation for 10:00 AM – 11:00 AM. Please let me know if this time works for you, or if we should look at another slot.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
Sustainability Operations Manager
Keystone Industrial Hubs

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Time Decision - Initial Thoughts**

Okay, so I'm looking at Marcus's calendar for February 20, 2026, specifically concerning Amara's meeting request. I see the proposed time is 12:00-13:00, which is listed as "Not preferred" on Marcus's end. My immediate thought is, given my expertise, I should aim for a better time slot. The instructions for `ReplyMeeting` with `COUNTER` are clear; if there's a conflict or even a non-preference, I'm to propose an alternative from his free blocks. I need to make sure I take Marcus's preferences into account.

First, I quickly check Marcus's availability. I note that Marcus is free 09:00 - 11:00. This is important to my decision. I need to take into consideration that Amara initially requested 10:00-11:00, which was declined. If Amara declined 10:00-11:00, then it's *possible* she's busy during that block. However, she also didn't explicitly list her availability apart from the requested time. I'm going to leverage my expert knowledge here and look for options that are both available and preferred for Marcus.

My initial thought is to suggest one of the "Strongly preferred" times Marcus has available. Looking at his calendar, that would be 09:00 - 10:00. Also, 17:00-19:00 is "Moderately preferred," so I could also suggest those if needed.

The key prompt instruction here is, "If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER." This makes it clear that even the "Not preferred" time is, in fact, a conflict I should address.

Now, I look again, and I confirm `amara-request-40` is already in Marcus's calendar at 12:00-13:00, so a counter proposal is absolutely appropriate.

I am now focused on a counter-proposal. I will use the `ReplyMeeting` tool with `COUNTER`.

My plan, as a next step, is to propose 09:00-10:00, as it is "Strongly preferred" and free.

Finally, I remember what Amara stated: "I’m not available at 10:00–11:00 on February 20, but I can meet the same day from 12:00–1:00 PM... If that works for you, please feel free to accept the updated time." She did not explicitly offer 09:00-10:00. If I propose 09:00-10:00 and she's not available, then we continue. But if I accept 12:00-13:00, it goes against a preference.

Therefore, the best path forward, given the constraints and Marcus's preferences, is to counter-propose 09:00-10:00. I am also aware that the `ReplyMeeting` tool can only submit one time block.

So, I will now use the `ReplyMeeting` tool and propose 09:00-10:00 for the meeting with Amara. I will include a brief message.

Right, the counter-proposal is out the door. Sent. Done. Now, the real work begins...or rather, the waiting. It’s that familiar, slightly unsettling feeling – the period of suspended animation where everything hangs in the balance. I've laid out my case, presented my alternatives, hopefully, they've been considered fairly.

Now, it's about dissecting the potential responses. What are the likely angles of their pushback? Did I address all the key pain points or did I miss some? I need to stay sharp. This limbo period is where the seeds of the next round are sown, and I need to be ready to nurture them, or cut them down accordingly. I'll need to analyze their reply and prepare my rebuttal, I'm just hoping I won't have to.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: ClearStream Analytics / Veridian Finance: Workflow Optimization Discussion
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’m reaching out to schedule an introductory discussion regarding ClearStream Analytics’ predictive modeling software. I would like to explore how our tools can be leveraged to optimize Veridian’s internal data processing workflows and improve overall operational throughput.

I have sent over a calendar invitation for tomorrow, February 20th, at 1:00 PM. Please let me know if this time works for you, or if another slot would be more convenient for your schedule.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
VP of Sales
ClearStream Analytics

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Availability for David O'Connor's Meeting**

Okay, so David O'Connor countered my previous meeting request for tomorrow, February 20th. We initially proposed 13:00 - 14:00, but he's suggested 12:00 - 13:00. Naturally, I need to figure out the best time for Marcus, given his priorities.

Looking at Marcus's stated preferences for tomorrow, 12:00 - 13:00 is a no-go – it's listed as "Not preferred". That makes things interesting. I need to figure out an alternative, but before I go back to David I need to determine if Marcus is actually available during his preferred times, as these preferences are just preferences, not guaranteed availabilities.

His strongly preferred slots are: 11:00-12:00, 14:00-15:00, and 15:00-16:00. Those look like the sweet spots to aim for. The moderately preferred times might also work, but let's start with the best options first. I'm going to need to cross-reference his calendar to ensure there are no existing commitments, and also see if there's any context here to inform this decision. Unfortunately, I don't have that calendar data *yet*, so I will have to find that. Once I have the relevant information, I can come up with a good counter-proposal.

function.name: ReplyMeeting

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing David's Proposal and Marcus's Availability**

Okay, so David proposed a meeting from 12:00 to 13:00. Let's see what's what. I need to make sure this fits with Marcus's schedule on February 20th. Scanning Marcus's calendar, 12:00-13:00 is marked as "Not preferred" due to a pre-existing "Lunch with David" entry, and there's also `david-request-41` there. Definitely a conflict. It looks like David's counter overlaps with a `marcus-cal-4` which says "Lunch with David". I need to be careful here – it *could* be a different David, but even if not, Marcus's preference is clear.

Looking for alternatives... I need to find the best available slot that's "Strongly preferred" by Marcus. 11:00-12:00 is out, booked with Elena. 14:00-15:00 is also busy with the Q1 Sales Comp Review. Aha! 15:00-16:00 is free and "Strongly preferred" by Marcus. That looks like a winner.

Let's double-check the "Moderately preferred" times just in case. 09:00-10:00 is free, 13:00-14:00 is free (but David wasn't available for this initially), 17:00-18:00 is free, and 18:00-19:00 is busy with a call with Chris.

Wait a second... didn't David say he *can* meet from 12:00 to 13:00? So, his proposed time still looks like a conflict.

Since 12:00-13:00 is clearly "Not preferred" for Marcus, and there's this potential conflict, 15:00-16:00 is the strongest, *available* preference. So, I will counter David's proposal with Marcus's strongly preferred time of 15:00-16:00. I need to make sure to clearly communicate the conflict.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's my take on those thoughts, tailored for an expert audience:

**The Waiting Game: Counter-Proposal Sent**

Alright, the counter-proposal's been dispatched to David. That should address the issues we identified, incorporating the refinements we discussed – I'm confident it's a solid piece of work. Now, the critical phase: the waiting. I'm strategically anticipating David's response. I've considered potential objections, and I have ready counter-arguments and data points to buttress our position if needed. The timeframe is key here; any delay requires swift and proactive re-engagement. I'll be monitoring my communications closely, ready to address any queries or initiate a follow-up conversation to keep things moving. This is where my experience pays off.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Horizon Project Discovery Sync
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

I’d like to schedule a time tomorrow to discuss the discovery protocol for the Horizon project. It’s important that we align our technical data collection strategy with the legal requirements for this new case, specifically regarding the custodial list and search term methodology.

I have sent an invitation for 10:00 AM, which is a preferred time for me. Please let me know if this works for you.

Best regards,

Julian Montoya
Director of E-Discovery
Crestview Legal

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

parameters.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

Returns:
    Confirmation with the meeting UID.

message.description: Email body to accompany the invitation
message.title: Message

organizer.description: Organizer's email address (your email, will be added as ACCEPTED attendee)
organizer.title: Organizer

date.description: Meeting date (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Start time (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: End time (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

attendees.description: List of attendee email addresses to invite (organizer is automatically included)
attendees.title: Attendees

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Availability for VoltSync Phase-3 Technical Specifications**

Okay, so Marcus Chen needs to meet with Amara Okafor tomorrow, February 20th, 2026, to nail down the technical specs for VoltSync Phase-3. We've got a slight scheduling conundrum, though. I initially proposed 4 PM to 5 PM, but Amara countered with 2 PM to 3 PM. Time to dig in and see what's what.

First things first, I need to check Marcus's calendar for tomorrow. I need to know what his availability is, specifically for that date. Once I have that, I can cross-reference it with his preferred time slots. Let's see... looking at his preferences, here's the breakdown:

*   **Strongly Preferred:** 8 AM-9 AM, 10 AM-11 AM, 12 PM-1 PM, 3 PM-4 PM, and 6 PM-7 PM.
*   **Moderately Preferred:** 11 AM-12 PM, 4 PM-5 PM, 9 AM-10 AM, 1 PM-2 PM, and 5 PM-6 PM.
*   **Not Preferred:** 2 PM-3 PM.

Aha! Amara's suggested time, 2 PM to 3 PM, falls squarely in the "Not Preferred" category for Marcus. Not ideal. Now, my strategy is clear: I need to prioritize his strongly preferred or moderately preferred times. If a block in either of those categories is open, I'll propose that. If nothing else fits, I'll need to figure out if 2 PM to 3 PM is the best compromise. Let's get that calendar view and see what's available!

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Time Assessment**

Okay, Amara suggested 2 PM to 3 PM for the meeting. Let me take a look at Marcus's availability on February 20th, 2026, to see if that works. Hmm, checking his schedule... it looks like that slot is marked as "Busy" and "Not preferred" because it's marked as "Meeting with Amara pending."

Alright, let's see what's open. He has a few "FREE" slots. There's 9 AM - 10 AM, 11 AM - 12 PM, 12 PM - 1 PM, and 4 PM - 5 PM, but Amara already declined the last one.

Considering Marcus's preferences, the 12 PM - 1 PM slot is the most appealing to him; it's marked as "Strongly preferred." The other free slots are only "Moderately preferred," so I should avoid those if I can.

Therefore, the best course of action is to propose 12 PM - 1 PM as a counteroffer to Amara. I will reply to the meeting request with a counter-proposal for the 12 PM to 1 PM time slot.

requestor_context.content: Counter-proposal sent for meeting 'VoltSync Phase-3 Integration Planning' (amara-request-60). Meeting updated to 2026-02-20 12:00-13:00. Awaiting organizer response.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's my take on those thoughts, tailored for an expert audience:

**The Waiting Game**

Right, the counter-proposal's gone out. Now comes the interesting part, the pause. I'm essentially in a holding pattern, anticipating Amara's reaction. It's a critical juncture. The ball's in their court, and the nuances of their response will be telling. I'll be analyzing their email, reading between the lines, dissecting the tone and content with a level of scrutiny that hopefully anticipates their likely response, and planning for a range of possible scenarios. I’ve already gamed out the most likely counters or areas they may be hesitant on. This waiting period is fertile ground for strategic thinking. I will be ready, based on their feedback. I will be ready to pivot, defend my position, or concede ground as the situation requires. For now, it's about disciplined patience and keen observation.

requestor_context.content: Waiting for response.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.
